items.title: Dezi Freeman's shipping container entered by YouTubers, police investigate

children.content: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

items.title: Legal action considered after Dezi Freeman property entry

children.content: Property owner considers legal action after people enter the Upper Murray property where Dezi Freeman was shot.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Victoria Police Freedom of Information requests face record delays

children.content: Victoria Police is processing Freedom of Information requests slower than at any point in its history, with the wait exceeding nine months.

image.altText: A close up of a woman's hand shuffling though papers.

items.title: Bus replacements mean some V/Line commuters can't get to work on time

children.content: Some regional commuters hoping to save on petrol are stuck in the slow lane as Victoria's free public transport offer coincides with rail maintenance along the Warrnambool/Geelong line.

gallery.altText: Two white buses that say "V/Line", one with a red stripe and one with purple panels, parked beside a road.
A large, tightly packed crowd gather around the two open doors of a train at an indoor station.
A man with grey hair and dark rimmed glasses with a white collar over his navy jumper rests his hand on a white picket fence.

thumbnail.altText: Two white buses that say "V/Line", one with a red stripe and one with purple panels, parked beside a road.
A large, tightly packed crowd gather around the two open doors of a train at an indoor station.
A man with grey hair and dark rimmed glasses with a white collar over his navy jumper rests his hand on a white picket fence.

items.title: If you think a river is for boaties, that's far from the reality in this region

children.content: The first-ever review into the Murray-Darling Basin Plan is underway, and the St George community wants a better deal.

image.altText: Gomeroi elders Ron Waters and Sam Saunders sit on top of the E.J Beardmore Dam near St George, Queensland, August 2023.

items.title: No police report filed over dramatic footage of boy being dragged by bus

children.content: Authorities are investigating how a 12-year-old became trapped in a bus door before being dragged hundreds of metres down a suburban street in Melbourne.

image.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

items.title: Blues confirm Hollands has been admitted to hospital after concerning scenes

children.content: Carlton says young star Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital after suffering a mental health episode in Thursday night's match.

image.altText: Elijah Hollands follows through after kicking a ball at Carlton training

items.title: Milk, bread prices to increase within weeks due to war in Iran

children.content: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

image.altText: A woman shopping in a grocery store aisle.

items.title: Alleged Google searches by accused cafe killer described at trial

children.content: The Supreme Court has been told of the alleged internet searches made by a man accused of a brazen daylight cafe shooting in Melbourne's north-west.

gallery.altText: A man in a cap and sweatshirt sitting on a park bench.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a cap and sweatshirt sitting on a park bench.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Garbage collectors and teachers plan strike action for budget week

children.content: More than 1,000 workers across seven local councils in Melbourne are set to walk off the job next month on the same day the Victorian government releases its budget, with garbage collection to be impacted by the strike.

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

image.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: Man refused bail with stealing seven vehicles, including unmarked police car

children.content: A Shepparton man has been remanded in custody accused of stealing seven cars in Melbourne and Victoria's north in March and April.

image.altText: Police officers apprehend a man whose face is blurred

items.title: Victoria's free public transport continues in May with half-price fares to follow

children.content: Public transport will be free in Victoria for another month, and fares will then be slashed by half until the rest of the year in a bid to curb fuel demand and provide economic relief.

image.altText: Passengers standing on a train platform as a train pulls in.

items.title: Man faces court over pedestrian death outside comic convention

children.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

gallery.altText: A prisoner, looking up, elbows resting on his legs

thumbnail.altText: A prisoner, looking up, elbows resting on his legs

items.title: Three months on from fires, the Natimuk community still faces challenges

children.content: State and federal governments give Natimuk and surrounding areas an extra $1.3 million to rebuild after devastating summer bushfires.

image.altText: Elderly gentleman starting at camera with bush fire impacted house reduced to rubble and chimneys behind him

items.title: How a park on the Murray carries our bonds with the Western Front

children.content: A local Australian artist is exploring the bonds between a regional park and its namesake in France as part of an Anzac Day exhibit.

image.altText: Black and white photograph of Albury soldiers standing in front of a French building

items.title: Triple murderer Erin Patterson's estranged husband writing his memoir

children.content: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

image.altText: Simon and Erin Patterson in a photo graphic in front of Ms Patterson's Leongatha home.

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

children.content: Elihu and Amanda Del Valle say thousands of dollars worth of their belongings were stolen from the boot of their car when it was shipped from Melbourne to Perth, but police aren't able to investigate.

image.altText: A close up photo of a man with dark hair, wearing a black top

items.title: Liberal candidate under investigation for roadworks ahead of Nepean by-election

children.content: The Liberal Party's candidate for the crucial upcoming Nepean by-election is being investigated by Victoria's transport department for unauthorised roadworks in a campaign stunt with Opposition Leader Jess Wilson.

gallery.altText: A drone shot shows an aerial image of a suburban landscape near a beach and bay area.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot shows an aerial image of a suburban landscape near a beach and bay area.

items.title: Man hospitalised after machete attack at Melbourne shopping mall

children.content: Police say a group of men approached and allegedly stabbed an 18-year-old man at the Eaton Mall in Oakleigh.

image.altText: A crime scene

items.title: Long before Dezi Freeman, other criminals hid along the Upper Murray

children.content: Dezi Freeman used Victoria's Upper Murray region to hide, but it has long been a popular location for those trying to avoid capture.

image.altText: a man in a flat cap holds a black and white newspaper clipping

items.title: Viva Energy says fire at Victorian refinery will not cause fuel supply issues

children.content: Despite the refinery running at reduced capacity following a fire, experts say the incident in Geelong this week should not significantly affect fuel prices.

image.altText: An oil refinery

items.title: Teen 'cannon fodder' hired in Melbourne arson spree, police say

children.content: Police suspect a series of arson attacks on Melbourne hospitality venues in the past week have been arranged by criminals paying young people to commit the crime.

image.altText: Police officers at a crime scene.

items.title: Authors turn to self-publishing as big publishers look to cut costs

children.content: Is your dream to become the next JK Rowling or Stephanie Meyer? A new wave of writers is turning to self-publishing to turn fantasy into reality.

gallery.altText: Artwork of a female warrior with a sword in front of a fire-breathing dragon.

thumbnail.altText: Artwork of a female warrior with a sword in front of a fire-breathing dragon.

items.title: 'Australians deserve to know': Victorian political donations site goes dark

children.content: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan touches her glasses while at a press conference.

items.title: Mayor fears hidden cameras may have been in town hall change rooms for years

children.content: The Horsham mayor is worried that hidden cameras reportedly found in the town hall theatre change rooms could have been there for years.

image.altText: Inside Horsham Town Hall foyer, turquoise wall in background, chairs and tables in foreground

items.title: Police investigating fatal multi-vehicle collision near Violet Town

children.content: The Hume Freeway remains closed southbound following a multi-vehicle crash near Violet Town.

items.title: 'Snacks' ordered for alleged hit men's escape after underworld killing, court hears

children.content: Victoria's Supreme Court is told underworld figure Gavin Preston was cautious about his surroundings and "never" sat outside a cafe he regularly attended — other than the day he was shot dead.

image.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

items.title: Attempted murder charges possible over Wangaratta stabbing

children.content: A man has been refused bail over an alleged stabbing incident that has left a 53-year-old woman in a critical condition with skull fractures and part of her ear severed.

image.altText: Blue and white police tape at a crime scene.

items.title: Poor communication, no updates during oil refinery fire, residents claim

children.content: Geelong residents near an oil refinery fire have told a community meeting that initial information from emergency services was misleading and there was a lack of updates.

articles.title: Oil refinery fire will not push Australia to fuel plan 'stage three', PM says

children.content: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

articles.title: PM rejects Trump's claim that US asked for help in Strait of Hormuz — as it happened

children.content: Anthony Albanese says the US made "no new requests" for Australia's assistance in the Strait of Hormuz, despite Donald Trump telling reporters he wasn't happy with Australia.

articles.title: The cause of the Viva Energy oil refinery fire under investigation. Here's what we know

children.content: Firefighters have managed to extinguish a blaze at one of Australia's major oil refineries. Here's what we know so far about the cause of the fire — and the possible impact on fuel prices.

image.altText: fire and smoke pictured above the Geelong refinery in the evening

gallery.altText: Signs at the Viva Energy facility in Geelong read 'Refinery under alarm'.

thumbnail.altText: Signs at the Viva Energy facility in Geelong read 'Refinery under alarm'.
